name: Jose Aldo
winProfile: Big signature wins, lighter FW volume

qualityWinLedger.context: Former UFC champion moving down while still elite and title-caliber.
Elite featherweight rematch win, with aging/repeat context below max.
Former title challenger and elite contender moving down.
Legit title challenger but softer than Aldo’s best UFC wins.
Strong ranked bantamweight win late in Aldo’s UFC run.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: McGregor, Holloway, Volk, and Yan did not end the window because Aldo kept re-proving elite relevance. Merab is the unrecovered endpoint.

lossContextEvents.context: Aldo returned to interim-title and elite contender relevance.
Immediate title rematch kept Aldo at championship level.
Aldo later rebuilt into elite bantamweight relevance.
Aldo moved to bantamweight and re-proved elite relevance.
Aldo remained title-level and fought Petr Yan for the bantamweight belt.
Munhoz and Font wins kept Aldo inside the canonical elite window.

shortCase: Aldo is a scope-affected UFC-only legend: strong title value, real longevity, and quality wins, but not the same case he has when WEC is included.
bestArgument: Aldo wins debates when the opponent has weaker championship proof or less sustained elite relevance. He loses more often when the opponent has a cleaner UFC-only prime or stronger modern quality wins.
counterArgument: Aldo’s argument is historical greatness plus longevity. If WEC is included, his case jumps. In this UFC-only ranking, that context is respected but not scored directly.
whyRankedHere: Aldo ranks in the top-15 range because the UFC-only model still respects his title work, quality wins, and ability to stay relevant across featherweight and bantamweight. He is a real legend, but the app no longer treats him like a clean top-10 UFC-only résumé.
whyNotHigher: The UFC-only boundary hurts him because his full historical peak includes WEC. Inside the UFC-only scoring window, the McGregor KO, Holloway TKO losses, Volkanovski loss, and uneven back half make him too damaged for the top ten.
finalTakeaway: Aldo is a scope-affected legend: historically massive, still very strong in UFC-only scoring, but more believable around the top-14/top-15 range than as a top-10 UFC-only GOAT.
